title: Invalid Redirect URL for Google OAuth
symptom: When configuring Google OAuth for n8n, an 'Invalid Redirect' error occurs, stating it must end with a public top-level domain.
applies_when: Running n8n locally with a non-public domain
not_this_if: Using a public domain for n8n
root_cause: Google requires the redirect URI to be a public Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN) with a valid top-level domain. Local addresses or those with ports do not meet this requirement, leading to validation errors during the OAuth setup.
fix_steps: Install ngrok and log in using the instructions from ngrok.com
Run the command 'ngrok http <your_n8n_port>' to generate a public HTTPS URL
Use the resulting URL as the redirect URL in your Google Cloud configuration
agent_instruction: Use ngrok to create a public HTTPS URL for your n8n instance and update the Google OAuth redirect URL accordingly.
